Small horses, big returns: Hedge’s weekend of karma

With eight stakes winners this season, a second Group 1 winner and three juvenile stakes winners, Trapeze Artist is building momentum on the track. Yet with his fee easing to $27,500 and his yearlings averaging $75,000 in 2026, the son of Snitzel remains priced like a stallion still waiting for the market to catch up.

Jockey Karis Teetan celebrated a treble led by the promising Mr Incredible who has Derby goals for next season. Fownes extended his lead on the Trainer’s Premiership with five meetings to go, while Toronado and Blue Point both celebrated doubles on the card at Sha Tin.

A hot field for the G1 Stephen Foster Stakes was left in the wake of Magnitude who was coming off winning the G1 Dubai World Cup last start. In Ireland, Estrange won the G1 Pretty Polly Stakes and will be aimed at the Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe.

Splash Back earned her first Group 1 in the Tatt's Tiara while Yellow Brick took his earnings over $4 million with a Group 3 victory. North Pacific celebrated his first stakes winner with Zuleika, and apprentice Siena Grima rode a treble at Rosehill to close the gap on the Sydney Apprentice Jockey's Title.

First season sire Stay Inside added his fourth stakes winner with unbeaten colt The Machine Gun, while the gun theme continued with Gun Runner colt Genesis Runner victorious at Newcastle. Apprentice Caitlin Tootell celebrated her first stakes win on Pretty Baby in South Australia.

With retirement possibly looming, Group 1 winner Manaal gets one final chance to add another elite-level victory in Saturday’s G1 Tattersall’s Tiara, while Cool Gent, Touristic, Coeur Volante and Mountjoy also headline a fascinating day of Winter racing across Australia.

With just over a month remaining in the 2025/26 Australian racing season, several jockey premierships remain undecided. While some leaders have established commanding advantages, others are locked in enthralling contests that promise to go right down to the wire.

Strong demand from a deep domestic and international buying bench fuelled an encouraging edition of New Zealand Bloodstock’s National Weanling Sale, with turnover, average and clearance rate all posting significant gains at Karaka on Thursday.
